science.title: Secure Data Transmission Reaches 1.9 Mbit/s Over 25km Fibre Optic Link
science.description: Previously, continuous-variable quantum key distribution relied on discrete components, hindering practical deployment. Now, a fully integrated silicon photonic transceiver, utilising polarisation multiplexing, transmits a secret key at 1. 9 Mbit/s over 25km of fibre.

science.title: Wave Solutions Decay Predictably In Complex Spaces
science.description: Researchers have determined a critical threshold for energy levels in wave equations, demonstrating that solutions with low energy persist indefinitely, while those exceeding it rapidly collapse, even with minimal initial disturbance.

science.title: Black Hole Interiors Reveal Limits To Known Physics At Extreme Scales
science.description: A breakdown of effective gravity now appears at length scales of $e^{S_0/3}$, a factor of $e^{2S_0}$ faster than previously predicted. This unexpected fragility stems from the contribution of rare, negative energy states within a dual boundary random matrix ensemble. Understanding this mechanism reshapes expectations for quantum gravity and black hole physics.

science.title: Quantum Particles Bypass ‘self-Trapping’ With New Simulation Technique
science.description: Two particles, seemingly held fast by an invisible barrier, can now be accurately modelled as they tunnel through it, a feat previously hampered by a persistent distortion in calculations. The time-dependent generator coordinate method overcomes this ‘self-trapping’ effect, aligning simulated quantum tunneling with known exact solutions. This advance promises more reliable modelling of nuclear reactions and fission processes.
